qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via Chromium URL conversion endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes generic SSRF via unrestricted http/https access to internal services, not the case-insensitive file:// scheme bypass described in the advisory. The advisory specifically concerns bypassing the file:// deny-list using mixed-case URL schemes (FILE://, File://, etc.) to read local files, while this finding focuses on http/https SSRF to cloud metadata endpoints and internal services. Different vulnerable code path and different security consequence.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory describes a case-insensitive URL scheme bypass in Gotenberg's Chromium deny-list (pkg/gotenberg/filter.go) that allows file:// URL access using mixed-case schemes (FILE://, File://, etc.). The only qualifying finding 'ssrf-chromium-url-conversion' describes generic http/https SSRF to internal services, not the file:// bypass. The finding does not reference the deny-list regex compilation, case-sensitivity issue, or file:// scheme bypass.
evidence: Advisory root cause: pkg/gotenberg/filter.go compiles deny-list regex with case-sensitive matching (regexp2.MustCompile with no (?i) flag), allowing FILE://, File://, etc. to bypass the ^file: pattern
Advisory vulnerability: Case-insensitive URI scheme bypass per RFC 3986 allowing local file read via Chromium PDF rendering
Finding 'ssrf-chromium-url-conversion': Focuses on unrestricted http/https access to internal services and cloud metadata endpoints, not file:// scheme bypass
